content.full_text:
DAVID BAUER BUSINESS SERVICES
1121 L Street, Suite 1000
Sacramento, CA 95814
Tel (916) 447-4489 · Fax (916) 441-6639


August 31, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St., Suite 800
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Ms Donovan:

A recipient committee plans to conduct a public opinion poll in a
legislative district to determine whether the committee should
become involved in an independent expenditure campaign in that
district. In addition to tallying the results of the survey,
the committee will update its voter list with the responses of
those surveyed.

If the survey results are not positive, the committee will not
participate in the campaign and would use the information, if at
all, for internal purposes only. In such an event, is the cost of
the survey an independent expenditure by the committee?

If the committee decides to become involved in the campaign
because of the survey results, does the cost of the survey become
an independent expenditure?

If the committee becomes involved in the campaign and sends mail
specifically targeted to those who responded to the survey, does
the cost of the survey or of adding the survey responses to the
voter list become an independent expenditure?

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

David Bauer
